name: msvg
description: Create, validate, inspect, and preview MotionSVG animation packages, and split SVG paths into subpaths — the msvg toolchain as MCP tools.
long_description: msvg-mcp exposes the MotionSVG (msvg) toolchain over the Model Context Protocol. It provides eight tools: scaffold a new animation package (msvg_create), validate it against the schema and its SVG (msvg_validate), inspect and summarize its structure (msvg_inspect, msvg_summarize), resolve target selectors (msvg_check_targets), run a local preview server (msvg_preview_start / msvg_preview_stop), and split an SVG path into per-part subpaths for animation (msvg_split_subpaths). All directory arguments are absolute paths.

tools.name: msvg_create
tools.description: Scaffold a new, valid MotionSVG animation package under an absolute parent directory.

tools.name: msvg_validate
tools.description: Validate a package against the msvg schema and its SVG; returns a structured issue list and pass/fail.

tools.name: msvg_inspect
tools.description: Print a human-readable overview of a package: assets, targets, timelines, and states.

tools.name: msvg_check_targets
tools.description: Resolve every target selector against assets/main.svg and report matches and failures.

tools.name: msvg_summarize
tools.description: Emit an AI-friendly Markdown summary of a package: safe files, names, and validation status.

tools.name: msvg_preview_start
tools.description: Start a local (127.0.0.1) preview server with a control panel for a package; returns the URL.

tools.name: msvg_preview_stop
tools.description: Stop one preview server by port, or all running previews.

tools.name: msvg_split_subpaths
tools.description: Split an SVG path `d` string (or an .svg file's first path) into absolutized subpaths for per-part animation.
